ud2.8:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Kuṇḍiya in the Kuṇḍiṭṭhāna forest.
ud2.8:1.3: And on that occasion Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter had been seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor.
ud2.8:1.4: She–touched by fierce, sharp pains–endured them with three thoughts:
ud2.8:1.5: “How rightly self-awakened is the Blessed One who, abandoning this sort of suffering, teaches the Dhamma!
ud2.8:1.6: How well-practiced is the community of the Blessed One’s disciples who practice, abandoning this sort of suffering!
ud2.8:1.7: How truly blissful is unbinding, where this sort of pain is not found!”
ud2.8:2.1: Then Suppavāsā said to her husband,
ud2.8:2.2:
“Come, young master.
Go to the Blessed One and, on arrival, showing reverence with your head to his feet in my name,
ask whether he is free from illness & affliction, is carefree, strong, & living in comfort, saying:
ud2.8:2.3:
‘Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, lord,
shows reverence with her head to your feet and asks whether you are free from illness & affliction,
are carefree, strong, & living in comfort.’
ud2.8:2.5: ‘Suppavāsā has been seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor.
ud2.8:2.6: She–touched by fierce, sharp pains–endures them with three thoughts:
ud2.8:2.7: “How rightly self-awakened is the Blessed One who, abandoning this sort of suffering, teaches the Dhamma!
ud2.8:2.8: How well-practiced is the community of the Blessed One’s disciples who practice, abandoning this sort of suffering!
ud2.8:2.9: How truly blissful is unbinding, where this sort of pain is not found!”’”
ud2.8:3.1:
Responding,
“Excellent!” to Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, the Koliyan-son went to the Blessed One and,
on arrival, having bowed down to the Blessed One, sat to one side.
ud2.8:3.2: As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
ud2.8:3.3:
“Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, lord,
shows reverence with her head to your feet and asks whether you are free from illness & affliction,
are carefree, strong, & living in comfort.
ud2.8:3.5: ‘Suppavāsā has been seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor.
ud2.8:3.6: She–touched by fierce, sharp pains–endures them with three thoughts:
ud2.8:3.7: “How rightly self-awakened is the Blessed One who, abandoning this sort of suffering, teaches the Dhamma!
ud2.8:3.8: How well-practiced is the community of the Blessed One’s disciples who practice, abandoning this sort of suffering!
ud2.8:3.9: How truly blissful is unbinding, where this sort of pain is not found!”’”
ud2.8:4.1:
[The Blessed One said:] “May Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter be well & free from disease.
And may she deliver a son free from disease.”
ud2.8:4.2:
And at the same time as the Blessed One’s statement, Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter–
well & free from disease–delivered a son free from disease.
ud2.8:5.1:
Saying,
“Very well, lord,” the Koliyan-son, delighting in & approving of the Blessed One’s words,
got up from his seat, bowed down to the Blessed One and–circling him to the right–returned to his home.
ud2.8:5.2: He saw that Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter–well & free from disease–had delivered a son free from disease.
ud2.8:5.3: On seeing this, the thought occurred to him,
ud2.8:5.4:
“How amazing!
How astounding!–the Tathāgata’s great power, great might, in that, at the same time as the Blessed One’s statement,
Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter–well & free from disease–would deliver a son free from disease!”
ud2.8:5.5: Gratified, he was joyful, rapturous, & happy.
ud2.8:6.1: Then Suppavāsā said to her husband,
ud2.8:6.2:
“Come, young master.
Go to the Blessed One and, on arrival, showing reverence with your head to his feet in my name, saying:
ud2.8:6.3: ‘Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, lord, shows reverence with her head to your feet.’
ud2.8:6.5: ‘Suppavāsā, who was seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor,
ud2.8:6.6: has now–well & free from disease–delivered a son free from disease.
ud2.8:6.7: She invites the community of monks, with the Buddha at its head, for seven days of meals.
ud2.8:6.8: May the Blessed One acquiesce to Suppavāsā’s seven meals, together with the community of monks.’”
ud2.8:7.1:
Responding,
“Excellent!” to Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, the Koliyan-son went to the Blessed One and,
on arrival, having bowed down to the Blessed One, sat to one side.
ud2.8:7.2: As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
ud2.8:8.1: “Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, lord, shows reverence with her head to your feet.
ud2.8:8.3: ‘Suppavāsā, who was seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor,
ud2.8:8.4: has now–well & free from disease–delivered a son free from disease.
ud2.8:8.5: She invites the community of monks, with the Buddha at its head, for seven days of meals.
ud2.8:8.6: May the Blessed One acquiesce to Suppavāsā’s seven meals, together with the community of monks.’”
ud2.8:9.1:
Now at that time a certain lay follower had invited the community of monks,
with the Buddha at its head, for the next day’s meal.
ud2.8:9.2: That lay follower was a supporter of Ven. Mahā Moggallāna.
ud2.8:9.3: So the Blessed One addressed Ven. Mahā Moggallāna,
ud2.8:9.4:
“Come, Moggallāna.
Go to the lay follower and, on arrival, say to him,
ud2.8:9.5: ‘Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, who was seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor,
ud2.8:9.6: has now–well & free from disease–delivered a son free from disease.
ud2.8:9.7: She has invited the community of monks, with the Buddha at its head, for seven days of meals.
ud2.8:9.8:
Let Suppavāsā do seven meals.
Afterward, you will do yours.’
ud2.8:10.1:
Responding,
“As you say, lord,” to the Blessed One, Ven. Moggallāna went to the lay follower and, on arrival, said to him,
ud2.8:10.2: “Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter, who was seven years pregnant and seven days in difficult labor,
ud2.8:10.3: has now–well & free from disease–delivered a son free from disease.
ud2.8:10.4: She has invited the community of monks, with the Buddha at its head, for seven days of meals.
ud2.8:10.5:
Let Suppavāsā do seven meals;
afterward, you will do yours.”
ud2.8:11.1: “Venerable sir, if Ven. Moggallāna will be my guarantor for three things–
ud2.8:11.2: (my) wealth, life, & faith–then let Suppavāsā do seven meals;
ud2.8:11.4: “For two things, friend, will I be your guarantor:
ud2.8:11.6: Only you are the guarantor of your faith.”
ud2.8:12.1: “Venerable sir, if Ven. Moggallāna will be my guarantor for two things–
ud2.8:12.2: (my) wealth & life–then let Suppavāsā do seven meals;
ud2.8:13.1: Then Ven. Moggallāna, having conciliated the lay follower, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said,
ud2.8:13.2: “The lay follower, lord, has been conciliated.
ud2.8:13.3:
Let Suppavāsā do seven meals;
afterward, he will do his.”
ud2.8:14.1:
So for seven days Suppavāsā the Koliyan-daughter with her own hand served & satisfied the community of monks,
with the Buddha at its head, with exquisite staple & non-staple food.
ud2.8:14.2: And she had the child show reverence to the Blessed One and the community of monks.
ud2.8:15.1: Then Ven. Sāriputta said to the child,
ud2.8:15.2:
“I trust, child, that things are bearable for you.
I trust that things are comfortable for you.
I trust that there’s no pain.”
ud2.8:15.3: “From where, Ven. Sāriputta, would things be bearable for me?
ud2.8:15.4: From where would they be comfortable for me living seven years in a belly of blood?”
ud2.8:16.2: Then Suppavāsā–(thinking,) “My son is conversing with the Dhamma General!”–was gratified, joyful, rapturous, & happy.
ud2.8:16.3: The Blessed One, knowing that Suppavāsā was gratified, joyful, rapturous, & happy, said to her,
ud2.8:16.4: “Suppavāsā, would you like to have another son like this?”
ud2.8:16.5: “Lord Blessed One, I would like to have seven more sons like this!”
ud2.8:17.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud2.8:18.1: The disagreeable in the guise of the agreeable,
ud2.8:18.2: the unlovable in the guise of the lovable,
ud2.8:18.3: pain in the guise of bliss,
